config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap dilindungi sebagai batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu didahulukan tanpa batas, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh negara atau mayoritas untuk menekan yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan mengorbankannya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya aman dan dihargai.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should always come before the individual, because that can easily become an excuse to silence people and ignore basic dignity. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value social stability and collective responsibility, and I understand why many people here think public order and national interests matter a lot. But I also believe individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because once rights are too weak, the "greater good" can be used to justify unfairness. The best balance is a society that protects common interests without sacrificing the person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu kalau dipahami secara mutlak, karena di Indonesia sendiri “kepentingan masyarakat” sering dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam suara kecil atau mengorbankan kelompok rentan. Memang ada hal-hal yang perlu didahulukan demi kepentingan umum, seperti kesehatan publik, keamanan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar individu. Bagi saya, yang sehat adalah keseimbangan yang jelas: masyarakat kuat justru kalau hak orang per orang dihormati, bukan ketika individu dipaksa selalu mengalah. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik tanpa batas, yang paling sering menang bukan masyarakat, melainkan mayoritas atau penguasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as written, because putting society first by default can easily turn into a tool for control and abuse. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective interests, public order, and social stability, and I do not dismiss those values at all. But I also think the recent point is important: when “the common good” is used too broadly, it can silence weaker voices and treat basic rights as optional. For me, society should sometimes take priority in specific emergencies, but individual rights must remain the baseline, not the exception.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu kalau dijadikan prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan minoritas atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Saya setuju ada situasi tertentu seperti bencana, wabah, atau keamanan publik yang menuntut pembatasan sementara terhadap kebebasan individu, tetapi itu harus sangat jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan permanen. Justru kalau hak individu dijaga sebagai dasar, masyarakat lebih kuat dan lebih adil, karena negara tidak punya ruang terlalu besar untuk bertindak semena-mena. Menurut saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tapi bukan di atas hak dasar manusia.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in practice “society first” can easily become a justification for pressure, censorship, or unfair treatment of weaker people. From my perspective as someone from China, I do understand why collective interests, stability, and public order are valued, and in real emergencies society may need temporary priority. But I do not think that should change the baseline: individual rights must be protected first, otherwise the idea of the common good can be used too loosely by authorities or the majority. A healthy society is not one where individuals are always asked to give up more, but one where public interests are pursued without making personal rights disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
